fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Texas Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Texas — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license texas, dental assistant certification texas, RDA texas, DANB CDA texas, dental assistant radiography texas

board.name: Texas State Board of Dental Examiners

license_types.title: Registered Dental Assistant (RDA)
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in Texas.

steps.title: Apply for State Credential
steps.description: Submit application to the Texas State Board of Dental Examiners with required documentation and fees.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Registered Dental Assistant (RDA) through the Texas State Board of Dental Examiners by registration.

faqs.question: Does Texas require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, Texas requires Registered Dental Assistant (RDA) through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Texas?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Texas.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Texas?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Texas?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $48. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Registered Dental Assistant (RDA) through the Texas State Board of Dental Examiners by registration.
